candidates.why: Explicit top-line operating revenues with substantial values across years; standard primary revenue line.
Alternative label for core operating revenues; currently shows nan but may be used in other years per data rule.
Includes revenues plus other income; suitable fallback if more specific revenue lines are missing for some years.
This is an operating expense (SG&A), not revenue; included only because listed but semantically incorrect for REVT.

candidates.row_label: Changes in fair value of derivatives, negative  (revenue/other income adj.)
candidates.why: Adjustment to revenue/other income, not the main revenue line; should not be primary REVT.

candidates.row_label: Changes in fair value of derivatives, positive  (revenue/other income adj.)
candidates.why: Adjustment to revenue/other income, not the main revenue line; should not be primary REVT.

candidates.row_label: Loss on sale of assets (revenue/other income adj.)
candidates.why: Specific adjustment item, not core revenue; inappropriate as REVT.

variables.notes: Revenues is the clear top-line. Operating Revenue and Total revenues and other income are provided as alternates in case of reporting label changes.

candidates.why: Inventory change adjustment often used in deriving cost of goods sold, but not itself labeled as COGS.
Explicitly labeled cost of goods sold with values; direct production cost line.
Balance sheet inventory component, not an expense; not appropriate for COGS.

variables.notes: Cost of goods sold is primary COGS. Changes in Inventory is included as a secondary option since some systems construct COGS from it when COGS is missing.

candidates.why: Broad operating overhead bucket excluding COGS; good SG&A proxy when detailed SG&A may be incomplete.
Classical SG&A-type expense; clearly an SG&A component.
Explicit SG&A line; values appear nan in preview but per rules still should be included as primary SG&A label.
Share-based compensation typically classified within SG&A; good SG&A component.

candidates.row_label: Gain on sale of assets (operating/administrative expenses)
candidates.why: This is a gain within operating/administrative expenses, not an ongoing SG&A expense; should be excluded from SG&A components.

variables.notes: Following rule (b): explicit SG&A row exists but appears empty, so include it plus broader operating expense buckets. Exclude the gain-on-sale line as it is not an SG&A expense.

candidates.why: Clearly labeled total R&D expenditure line; sole and appropriate candidate for XRD.
Interest income, opposite sign of interest expense; not chosen for XINT which targets expense.
Generic label but appears unused (nan); possible legacy label for interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest expenses and other financial expenses
candidates.why: Aggregated line that includes interest expense; good fallback when pure interest expense not available.
Interest expense specifically on lease liabilities; component of total interest expense.
Pure interest expense line (with data) before capitalization; best primary candidate for XINT.
Broad finance expense category; reasonable fallback if more specific interest expense lines are missing.
Earnings attribution, not an interest expense; should not be used for XINT.
Same as attributable to non-controlling interests; not interest expense.
Interest capitalized to assets, not the expense recognized in P&L; should not be used directly for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental detail of interest on finance lease liabilities; good component of interest expense.
Balance sheet asset; explicitly non-interest-bearing; not relevant to interest expense.
Equity item, not an interest expense.
Equity item, not an interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest expenses and other financial expenses
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Ordered as a priority list: primary pure interest expense, then broader or component expense lines, then older generic label. Income, equity, and balance-sheet items are excluded.

candidates.row_label: Net income/(loss) from equity accounted investments
candidates.why: Income from associates, not equity; not a stock measure of book equity.

candidates.row_label: Attributable to equity holders of the company
candidates.why: Income attributable to equity holders, not the equity balance; not suitable for BE.

candidates.row_label: Share of OCI from equity accounted investments
candidates.why: OCI share from associates; not book equity.

candidates.row_label: Attributable to the equity holders of the company
candidates.why: Same as above, an income/OCI attribution, not the equity stock.
Profits from equity accounted investments; not the equity balance.
Asset representing investments in associates; not total shareholders’ equity.

candidates.row_label: Payables to equity accounted companies and other related parties
candidates.why: Liability to related parties; not equity.
Labeled shareholders’ equity, a proper book equity measure.
OCI component, not equity balance.
Explicit total equity including all components; best representation of book equity (BE).

variables.notes: Total Equity is preferred as it captures full book equity including minority interest if present; Shareholders equity is included as a fallback label.

candidates.why: Income attributable to NCI, not the equity balance; more of a flow measure but still indicates presence of minorities.
Same income attribution; again a flow, not the equity stock.
Balance sheet line for non-controlling interests’ equity; primary candidate for MIB.
Alternative label for non-controlling interests in equity; good secondary candidate.

candidates.row_label: Total Comprehensive Income attributable to Minority Interest
candidates.why: Flow of total comprehensive income to minorities, not the equity stock; not ideal for MIB.

variables.notes: MIB targets the equity stake of minority interests; therefore the balance sheet NCI/Minority Interest lines are used rather than income attribution rows.
